canonical_definition: The behavior of multi-turn dialogue generation is defined in the provided literature as the production of a sequence of user queries and model responses across multiple conversational turns. A central aspect of this behavior is the maintenance of coherence and alignment with an overarching intent throughout the interaction. One paper describes a framework, "Skeleton-Guided Multi-Turn Dialogue Generation," which operationalizes this by constraining instruction synthesis through the explicit modeling of human conversational intent. This process involves generating a "globally coherent sequence of user queries conditioned on the underlying intent." Subsequently, the model produces agent responses that are designed to "maintain alignment with both current and global conversational objectives."
papers: CAIR: Counterfactual-based Agent Influence Ranker for AgenticAIWorkflows
Expanding before Inferring: Enhancing Factuality in Large Language Models through Premature Layers Interpolation
Robust Native Language Identification through Agentic Decomposition
seqBench: A Tunable Benchmark to Quantify Sequential Reasoning Limits ofLLMs

definitions.text: Producing a sequence of user queries and model responses across multiple conversational turns, maintaining coherence and alignment with an overarching intent.
definitions.paper: Robust Native Language Identification through Agentic Decomposition
